ham.subject: Company Leave Policy Update – Effective 1st May 2026
ham.body:
Dear Team,

We are pleased to announce an update to our company leave policy effective from 1st May 2026. The key changes are:

1. **Annual Leave** – All full‑time employees will receive a total of 22 working days of paid annual leave per calendar year, up from 20 days.
2. **Sick Leave** – A maximum of 12 paid sick days per year, with an additional 5 days for long‑term illnesses (to be verified by a medical report).
3. **Maternity & Paternity Leave** – Maternity leave will be extended to 26 weeks with 100% salary coverage. Paternity leave will now be 4 weeks.
4. **Unpaid Leave** – Employees may apply for up to 30 days of unpaid leave per year, subject to manager approval.
5. **Leave Accrual** – Leave accrues monthly on the last working day of each month.

**How to Apply**
- Submit your leave request through the HR portal by 5:00 PM on the Friday before the intended leave date.
- Attach any required documentation (e.g., medical certificates) when applying for sick or maternity leave.
- Managers will review and approve or request additional information within 48 hours.

For detailed policy guidelines, please refer to the employee handbook on the intranet or contact HR@company.com with any questions.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Best regards,
Human Resources

spam.subject: URGENT: IT Helpdesk Ticket - Immediate Action Required
spam.body:
Hi <Employee Name>,

This is a reminder that your IT helpdesk ticket (Ticket# 839274) regarding the "Password Reset & Account Lockout" issue has not been resolved. According to our logs, your account has been flagged for multiple failed login attempts and is at risk of being permanently disabled.

To secure your account and prevent any potential data breach, you **must** verify your identity by clicking the link below within the next 24 hours:

<URL>

If you do not complete this verification, your access to all corporate resources (email, VPN, intranet) will be revoked and a temporary lock will be placed on your account. This is a mandatory security protocol to protect both you and the company.

Please note: Failure to comply may result in suspension of your access and could affect your employment status.

Thank you for your immediate attention to this matter.

Regards,
IT Helpdesk Team
